2024-04-26.thesis: ADVANCE Act nuclear licensing bill advances in Congress
2024-04-26.exit_case: none — ADVANCE Act had not yet been signed into law as of this date; still moving through Congress
2024-04-26.assessment: Entry week. ADVANCE Act is a bipartisan bill streamlining NRC licensing for advanced reactors, still advancing through Congress (not yet passed Senate/House or signed by President as of 4/26/24). SMR (NuScale) is the only NRC-design-approved US SMR developer and the cleanest pure-play, benefiting from both the bill's momentum and AI-driven power demand tailwind. Concrete next steps remain ahead (further congressional votes, eventual signing), so this is early-stage, not yet mainstream-saturated or resolved.
2024-04-26.exit_advice: Hold; exit only when Act is signed into law (priced) or licensing reform is reversed/stalled indefinitely

2024-07-12.thesis: ADVANCE Act nuclear licensing bill advances in Congress
2024-07-12.exit_case: President signed ADVANCE Act into law 7/9; the discrete legislative catalyst has fully resolved.
2024-07-12.assessment: The ADVANCE Act catalyst has completed its arc: Senate passage (6/18) -> presidential signature (7/9), confirmed by energy.gov and multiple financial outlets. This is exactly the acute, datable resolution the framework requires exiting on - the driving condition (bill pending in Congress) no longer exists; it is now law. Fool.com's coverage explicitly frames this as the legislation event driving today's pop, and the energy.gov headline itself flags 'buy-the-rumor-sell-the-news' - textbook priced-in/resolved signal. No concrete next legislative milestone remains for this specific catalyst (NRC rulemaking implementation is a separate, diffuse regulatory process, not a scheduled datable event this thesis was built on). Structurally, broader nuclear/SMR/AI-power-demand themes may continue, but the SPECIFIC catalyst entered on - the bill advancing/passing/signing - is now fully resolved and public. Per rules, catalyst_resolved=true mandates thesis_live=false regardless of continued share strength.
2024-07-12.exit_advice: Exit. Bill signed into law 7/9 - catalyst fully resolved and public; headline itself notes 'buy-the-rumor-sell-the-news' dynamic on SMR. Any further upside is now broader AI-power-demand/uranium theme, not this specific bill catalyst. Close position.
